title: Coverage Certificates for Parameter-Space Verification: Combining Statistical Sampling with Symbolic Proofs
approach_summary: We present a two-tier verification engine for XR spatial accessibility. Tier 1 is an interval-arithmetic linter that uses affine arithmetic to compute conservative reachability envelopes for parameterized 7-DOF kinematic chains, providing real-time (<2s) developer feedback in the Unity editor. Tier 2 is a sampling-guided symbolic engine that combines adaptive stratified sampling over the anthropometric parameter space with targeted linearized-kinematics SMT verification (Z3 on QF_LRA), producing coverage certificates that bound the probability of undetected accessibility bugs. The crown jewel is the coverage certificate formal object C = <S, V, U, epsilon, delta, kappa>, which composes stratified-sampling Hoeffding bounds with interval-verified and SMT-verified regions to achieve tighter guarantees than pure Monte Carlo. The theory addresses the piecewise-Lipschitz structure of the accessibility frontier (caused by joint-limit discontinuities) via explicit violation tracking and kappa-completeness metrics. A key synthesis insight is that Tier 1's affine-arithmetic green regions provide substantial symbolically verified volume (30-60% of parameter space), far exceeding the negligible volumetric contribution of SMT queries. The system targets enterprise XR accessibility compliance (Section 508, ADA Title I) with a two-paper strategy: CAV for the certificate theory, UIST for the developer tool.

definitions.name: XR Accessibility Scene Model
definitions.formal_statement: An XR accessibility scene is a tuple Omega = (E, G, D) where E = {e_1, ..., e_m} is a finite set of interactable elements (each with position p_i in R^3, activation volume V_i subset R^3 compact semialgebraic, interaction type tau_i, actuator a_i, required pose region r_i subset SE(3)); G = (E, Dep) is a directed acyclic interaction dependency graph; and D = {d_1, ..., d_p} is a finite set of target device configurations (each with tracking volume T_j, supported interactions I_j, movement mode M_j, controller transform c_j).
definitions.load_bearing: Every algorithm operates on this scene model. Parsing, verification, and certificate assembly all depend on D1's structure.

definitions.name: Parameterized Kinematic Body Model
definitions.formal_statement: A parameterized kinematic body model is B = (Theta, K, J, FK) where Theta subset R^d is the body parameter space (compact, d=5 ANSUR-II parameters: stature, arm length, shoulder breadth, forearm length, hand length); K(theta) is a 7-DOF kinematic chain (3-DOF shoulder, 1-DOF elbow, 3-DOF wrist) with link lengths l_i(theta) affine in theta; J(theta) = prod_{i=1}^7 [q_{i,min}(theta), q_{i,max}(theta)] subset T^7 is the joint limit set with limits affine in theta; and FK: Theta x T^7 -> SE(3) is forward kinematics FK(theta, q) = T_base(theta) * prod_{i=1}^7 R_i(q_i) * T_i(theta).
definitions.load_bearing: FK is evaluated in Tier 1 (affine arithmetic), Tier 2 (point evaluation via Pinocchio), and SMT encoding (linearized). Joint limits define the piecewise-Lipschitz partition.

definitions.name: Reachable Workspace
definitions.formal_statement: The reachable workspace of body theta is W(theta) = {FK(theta, q) | q in J(theta)} subset SE(3). Position projection: W_pos(theta) = {pos(FK(theta, q)) | q in J(theta)} subset R^3.
definitions.load_bearing: Accessibility predicates (D4) are defined via workspace intersection with activation volumes.

definitions.name: Accessibility Predicates
definitions.formal_statement: Single-step: Acc(e, theta, d) iff exists q in J(theta) such that pos(FK(theta,q)) in V_e cap T_d and FK(theta,q) in r_e and tau_e in I_d. Multi-step (k<=3): Acc_k(pi, theta, d) for sequence pi = (e_1,...,e_k) requires sequential reachability with feasible transitions (linear interpolation within J(theta)). Population: PAcc(e, Theta_target, D) iff forall theta in Theta_target, forall d in D: Acc(e, theta, d).
definitions.load_bearing: Every verification tier checks these predicates. Certificate error bounds are defined relative to acc(e,d,theta) = 1[Acc(e,theta,d)].

definitions.name: Accessibility Frontier Regularity (Piecewise-Lipschitz)
definitions.formal_statement: The accessibility function acc(e,d,.) : Theta -> {0,1} has a piecewise L-Lipschitz frontier if there exists a finite partition Theta = Theta_1 cup ... cup Theta_N such that acc is constant on each cell interior, and each inter-cell boundary is an L-Lipschitz codimension-1 manifold. Cell boundaries are contained in joint-limit transition surfaces: {theta | exists j: q_{j,max}(theta) = q_j*(e)} for critical angles q_j*(e). The number of surfaces is bounded by 2*|E|*n_joints.
definitions.load_bearing: C1 soundness requires this regularity. Without it, the Hoeffding bound cannot interpolate between samples. The piecewise formulation (replacing global Lipschitz) was mandated by Red-Team Attack 1.1.

definitions.name: Chart Embedding for SE(3) Predicates
definitions.formal_statement: Position predicates use pos: SE(3) -> R^3 directly. Orientation constraints use axis-angle within a single chart: ||log(R_0^{-1} * R(theta,q))|| <= alpha for target orientation R_0 and tolerance alpha < pi. Tier 1 over-approximates by ignoring orientation (sound). Tier 2 linearizes orientation constraints via C2.
definitions.load_bearing: Enables semialgebraic formulation of SE(3) predicates required for SMT encoding.

definitions.name: Coverage Certificate
definitions.formal_statement: A coverage certificate is C = <S, V, U, epsilon_analytical, epsilon_estimated, delta, kappa> where: S = {(theta_j, v_j)} is a sample set with pointwise verdicts; V = {(R_i, proof_i)} is verified regions including both SMT-backed proofs and Tier 1 affine-arithmetic green classifications; U = {(H_l, mu_l)} is unverified violation surfaces with neighborhood measures; epsilon_analytical uses conservative L_max from kinematic Jacobian; epsilon_estimated uses cross-validated L-hat; delta is confidence; kappa = mu(union Neps(H_l))/mu(Theta_target) is excluded fraction. Full certificate (kappa<=0.10), Partial (0.10<kappa<=0.30), Weak (kappa>0.30).
definitions.load_bearing: The certificate is the primary deliverable of Tier 2 and the core contribution of the CAV paper.

definitions.name: Linearized Kinematic Approximation
definitions.formal_statement: For reference (theta_0, q_0), the Taylor approximation is FK_lin(theta,q) = FK(theta_0,q_0) + J_theta*(theta-theta_0) + J_q*(q-q_0) where J_theta = dFK/dtheta and J_q = dFK/dq. The soundness envelope Delta_max satisfies: for all (theta,q) with ||theta-theta_0||_inf <= Delta_theta and ||q-q_0||_inf <= Delta_q, the linearization error is bounded by C2.
definitions.load_bearing: Enables SMT encoding as QF_LRA (linear real arithmetic) within the soundness envelope.

theorems.name: Coverage Certificate Soundness (Piecewise-Lipschitz)
theorems.statement: Let Omega be a well-formed XR scene, B a parameterized kinematic model, Theta_target compact. Under assumptions: (A1) piecewise L-Lipschitz frontier with known partition boundaries; (A2) identified violation surfaces H_1,...,H_{N_u} containing all partition boundaries; (A3) sufficient sampling density n_min = ceil((1/(2*eps^2)) * ln(2*|Strata|*|E|*|D|/delta)) per stratum in Theta_smooth = Theta_target \ union Neps(H_l); (A4) SMT verification soundness for QF_LRA; (A5) compactness of Theta_target. Then: P_sampling[exists theta in Theta_smooth \ union R_i, exists (e,d): cert_verdict != acc(e,d,theta)] <= epsilon with probability >= 1-delta. The excluded fraction is bounded: kappa <= sum_l 2*eps_l * L_l^{d-1} * Area(H_l cap Theta_target) / mu(Theta_target).
theorems.proof_sketch: Step 1: Union bound over elements, devices, strata. Step 2: Per-stratum Hoeffding on smooth sub-stratum (acc is continuous within each piecewise-Lipschitz cell by A1). Step 3: Volume accounting — verified regions V (Tier 1 green + SMT proofs) reduce effective unverified fraction rho_s per stratum. Step 4: Global epsilon = max over strata of rho_s * sqrt(ln(2K/delta)/(2*n_s)) where K = |E|*|D|*|Strata|. The proof is a careful composition of known tools (Hoeffding, union bound) applied to the novel setting of piecewise-Lipschitz kinematic parameter spaces with mixed sampling-symbolic verification.
theorems.novelty: Application of stratified sampling bounds to deterministic parameter-space verification (not stochastic temporal properties as in Younes-Simmons); integration of interval-verified regions into the bound; piecewise-Lipschitz handling of joint-limit discontinuities. Individual components known; composition is new.
theorems.load_bearing: Without C1, the certificate is 'Monte Carlo with SMT on the side' — no formal guarantee, no publication story. C1 is the crown jewel.
fragile_steps: Lipschitz constant estimation (circularity mitigated by dual-epsilon)
Stratum count entering logarithmically but affecting per-stratum sample count
Volume accounting for Tier 1 green regions requires Tier 1 soundness

theorems.name: Linearization Soundness Envelope
theorems.statement: For an n-joint revolute chain with link lengths l_i <= L_max, at reference (theta_0, q_0): ||FK(theta,q) - FK_lin(theta_0,q_0)(theta,q)||_2 <= C_FK * (Delta_q^2 + Delta_theta * Delta_q) * L_sum where C_FK = n/2, L_sum = sum l_i. For 7-DOF arm (n=7, L_sum ~ 0.7m) and eta = 1cm tolerance: Delta_q ~ 0.032 rad ~ 1.8 degrees, Delta_theta ~ 0.045 normalized.
theorems.proof_sketch: Taylor remainder theorem applied to kinematic chain composition. Hessian bounded by ||H_FK|| <= sum l_i * n_downstream(i) <= n * L_sum. Cross terms from mixed partials give the Delta_theta * Delta_q term. Standard technique with explicit constant C_FK = n/2 for revolute chains.
theorems.novelty: Explicit constant C_FK = n/2 for revolute chains. Technique is standard Taylor remainder; application to accessibility SMT encoding is new.
theorems.load_bearing: Determines SMT query granularity. Without C2, linearized SMT encoding has no soundness guarantee.
fragile_steps: C_FK = n/2 may be loose — empirical measurement could tighten

theorems.name: Budget Allocation Optimality
theorems.statement: Given compute budget T, the optimal allocation minimizing epsilon is: epsilon(N_samples, N_smt) = rho_effective * sqrt(ln(2K/delta) / (2*N_samples/|Strata|)) where rho_effective accounts for verified volume. Under volume-subtraction model: optimal alpha ~ 0.05% SMT. Under frontier-resolution model: alpha ~ 10-20%. The objective is quasi-convex, admitting binary search.
theorems.proof_sketch: Single-variable optimization. Substitute N_smt = (T - t_fk*N_samples)/t_smt. Differentiate epsilon w.r.t. N_samples. Show quasi-convexity from the sqrt and monotone rho_effective. Frontier-resolution model is conditional (unproven; tested at D3).
theorems.novelty: Application to certificate optimization with frontier-resolution accounting. Known optimization technique.
theorems.load_bearing: Guides runtime resource allocation. Without it, use heuristic — certificate still works but potentially looser.

theorems.name: Tier 1 Completeness Gap Bound
theorems.statement: For wrapping factor w >= 1, Tier 1 correctly classifies element e if margin(e,theta) > (w^{1/3} - 1) * diam(W_pos(theta)). Max undetectable bug radius: r_max = (w^{1/3} - 1) * diam(W_pos). With s subdivisions per dimension: r_max(s) = (w^{1/s^d})^{1/3} - 1) * diam(W_pos)/s. For w=5, s=4 in 3D: r_max ~ 2.5mm (practical precision).
theorems.proof_sketch: Geometric argument: affine-arithmetic enclosure E satisfies W_pos subset E with vol(E) <= w*vol(W_pos). Hausdorff distance between boundaries bounded by (w^{1/3}-1)*r. Subdivision reduces wrapping super-exponentially.
theorems.novelty: Connection between wrapping factor and accessibility detection margin. Geometric argument is elementary; domain application is new.
theorems.load_bearing: Characterizes Tier 1 precision. Drives subdivision depth choice. Used to determine which elements need Tier 2.
fragile_steps: Spherical approximation may not hold for elongated workspaces

theorems.name: Affine-Arithmetic Wrapping Factor for Revolute Chains
theorems.statement: For k-joint revolute chain with symmetric ranges Delta_i, wrapping factor w <= prod_{i=1}^k (1 + c_i * Delta_i^2) where c_i = (1/2)*(l_i / sum_{j>=i} l_j). For uniform Delta and equal links: w <= (1 + Delta^2/(2k))^k ~ exp(Delta^2/2). Numerical: k=7, Delta=30deg: w~1.15; k=7, Delta=45deg: w~1.36; k=7, Delta=60deg: w~1.71.
theorems.proof_sketch: Affine-arithmetic error propagation. Chebyshev linearization of sin/cos gives per-joint noise |eta_i| <= Delta_i^2/2. Noise propagates through downstream chain with amplification by downstream length. Per-joint multiplicative wrapping factor (1 + c_i*Delta_i^2).
assumptions: Chebyshev affine approximation of trig functions
theorems.novelty: Tight per-chain wrapping analysis with explicit multiplicative structure. Prior work (Stolfi & de Figueiredo 2003) gives generic bounds; kinematic chain specialization is new.
theorems.load_bearing: Predicts Tier 1 false-positive rate. Kill gate D1 validates this.
fragile_steps: Bilinear cross-term accumulation in matrix chain not fully captured — Red-Team estimates 4-7x for realistic 7-joint chains

assumptions.statement: XR interaction graphs have bounded treewidth (tw <= 6 for typical scenes)
assumptions.what_breaks_if_false: Compositional decomposition degrades; verification exponential in tw
assumptions.mitigation: Report tw per scene; degrade gracefully to monolithic for high-tw subgraphs

assumptions.statement: Interactions are k-local with k <= 4 joints
assumptions.what_breaks_if_false: Wrapping factor grows with effective DOF count
assumptions.mitigation: Subdivision handles higher-DOF; not fatal for sampling-based approach

assumptions.what_breaks_if_false: Certificate quality degrades (trajectory dimension k*d too high)
assumptions.mitigation: Hard limit; longer sequences reported as not certifiable

assumptions.statement: Activation volumes are compact semialgebraic sets
assumptions.mitigation: Enforce at parse time; approximate non-semialgebraic by bounding boxes

assumptions.statement: Piecewise-Lipschitz accessibility frontier with identifiable partition boundaries
assumptions.what_breaks_if_false: C1 epsilon bound invalid outside smooth cells
assumptions.mitigation: Detect violations, exclude neighborhoods, report kappa-completeness

assumptions.statement: Lipschitz constant L is estimable from samples or derivable from kinematic Jacobian
assumptions.what_breaks_if_false: L underestimated -> unsound; L overestimated -> epsilon too loose
assumptions.mitigation: Dual-epsilon: analytical L_max (sound but loose) + cross-validated L-hat (tight but not provably sound)

assumptions.statement: Linearization error bounded by C2 (C_FK = n/2)
assumptions.mitigation: Validate C2 empirically on 10K random configurations; use conservative eta

assumptions.statement: Joint limits are axis-aligned boxes in joint space
assumptions.what_breaks_if_false: Over-approximation of J(theta); conservative but sound
assumptions.mitigation: Larger joint-limit boxes ensure accessibility soundness
Use certified Z3 proofs (LFSC) when available
Compensated summation; extended-precision validation for 1% of samples

assumptions.statement: ANSUR-II representative of target XR user population
assumptions.mitigation: Document limitation; supplement with disability-specific ROM data

assumptions.statement: 7-DOF arm model captures relevant accessibility dimensions
assumptions.mitigation: Acknowledge as limitation; extend in future work

algorithms.name: Affine-Arithmetic Forward Kinematics
algorithms.pseudocode_summary: Initialize affine forms for joint angles (7 noise symbols) and body params (5 noise symbols). Propagate through chain: for each joint, compute aa_cos/aa_sin via Chebyshev linearization, accumulate rotation matrix (3x3 affine), add link contribution. Extract bounding box from final affine position forms.
algorithms.input: Joint-angle ranges [q_min, q_max], body parameter ranges [theta_min, theta_max]
algorithms.output: Axis-aligned bounding box enclosure of end-effector positions
algorithms.time_complexity: O(2^s * k * n^2) where s=subdivision levels (<=6), k=7 joints, n~20 noise symbols
algorithms.space_complexity: O(n) per affine form, O(9n) for rotation matrix
algorithms.expected_runtime: ~0.2ms per element without subdivision; ~10ms for 50-element scene with subdivision

implementation.risk: Medium: wrapping factor may exceed target on 7-joint chains with wide ranges

algorithms.synthesis_modifications: Mandatory joint-angle subdivision for ranges >45 degrees (synthesis Decision 3)

algorithms.name: Subdivided Affine FK with Accessibility Check
algorithms.pseudocode_summary: Estimate wrapping; if exceeds max_wrapping, find worst noise-symbol axis, bisect along it. Recurse up to max_subdivisions=6. Then intersect enclosure union with activation volume and tracking volume. Classify: GREEN (V subset W), RED (V cap W = empty), YELLOW (boundary; estimate excluded population fraction).
algorithms.input: Same as ALG1 plus max_wrapping threshold, element activation volume
algorithms.time_complexity: O(m * 2^6 * k * n^2) for m elements
algorithms.theorem_dependency: C4 (gap bound determines when YELLOW is reported)
algorithms.synthesis_modifications: GREEN results feed into certificate V set (synthesis Decision 7)

algorithms.name: Adaptive Stratified Sampling
algorithms.pseudocode_summary: Phase 1: Create strata from Tier 1 frontier (YELLOW regions = HIGH priority) plus uniform grid (3^5=243 background strata). Phase 2: Initial uniform LHS sampling (30% budget). Phase 3: Estimate local Lipschitz from sample pairs near frontier. Phase 4: Adaptive refinement — allocate remaining 70% proportional to frontier uncertainty * L-hat. Phase 5: Cross-validate L-hat with 20% holdout.
algorithms.input: Scene, parameter space, Tier 1 results, sample budget N, confidence delta
algorithms.output: Sample set S with verdicts, frontier estimate, Lipschitz estimates per stratum
algorithms.time_complexity: O(N * m * k) where N=samples, m=elements, k=FK cost
algorithms.theorem_dependency: C1 (sampling density requirement n_min per stratum)
algorithms.synthesis_modifications: Frontier seeding from Tier 1 YELLOW regions; marginal stratification only (d=5 body params) for multi-step

algorithms.name: Linearized-Kinematics SMT Encoding
algorithms.pseudocode_summary: At reference (theta_0, q_0): compute Jacobians J_theta and J_q analytically. Compute soundness envelope Delta_max from C2 bound with eta = margin/2. Build SMT formula in QF_LRA: declare delta_theta and delta_q variables, bound by Delta_max and joint limits, encode linearized position p_lin = p_0 + J_theta*d_theta + J_q*d_q, check p_lin in expanded activation volume. Query 1: SAT(p_lin in V_expanded) — if UNSAT, VERIFIED_INACCESSIBLE. Query 2: SAT(p_lin not in V_shrunk) — if UNSAT, VERIFIED_ACCESSIBLE. Timeout at 2s.
algorithms.input: Reference configuration, soundness radius, element, element/device indices
algorithms.output: VERIFIED_ACCESSIBLE | VERIFIED_INACCESSIBLE | UNKNOWN, plus SMT proof
algorithms.time_complexity: O(timeout) = 2s worst case; O(100ms) expected for QF_LRA

implementation.risk: Medium: query time variability; volume per query is negligible

algorithms.theorem_dependency: C2 (soundness envelope), C1 A4 (SMT soundness assumption)
algorithms.synthesis_modifications: SMT value reframed as frontier resolution, not volume elimination (synthesis Decision 2). Volume negligibility confirmed independently by Algorithm Designer and Red-Team.

algorithms.name: Coverage Certificate Assembly
algorithms.pseudocode_summary: Phase 1: Detect Lipschitz violations — for each (element, joint), find critical angles q_j*(e) where joint limit = required angle; compute violation surface hyperplanes and neighborhood measures. Phase 2: Compute Theta_smooth = Theta_target minus violation neighborhoods; compute kappa = excluded fraction. Phase 3: Per-stratum epsilon via Hoeffding with Bonferroni (K = |E|*|D|*|Strata|). Phase 4: Include Tier 1 GREEN regions in verified set V (synthesis Decision 7). Phase 5: Optional frontier-resolution enhancement for SMT queries. Phase 6: Assemble certificate with dual epsilon (analytical + estimated) and kappa.
algorithms.input: Sample set S, verified regions V (SMT + Tier 1), Lipschitz estimates, strata, scene, delta
algorithms.output: Certificate <S, V, U, eps_analytical, eps_estimated, delta, kappa>
algorithms.synthesis_modifications: Tier 1 green regions in V (Decision 7); dual epsilon (Decision 8); kappa-completeness tiers (Decision 6)

algorithms.name: Budget Allocation
algorithms.pseudocode_summary: Grid search over SMT fraction alpha in [0, 0.50] with step 0.01. For each alpha: compute N_samples and N_smt from time budget. Estimate epsilon under volume-subtraction model. Select alpha minimizing epsilon. Default ~5% to SMT. At runtime, adaptive: after 50 SMT queries, measure frontier-resolution rate; if >2x volume model, increase alpha toward frontier-resolution optimum. Compute SMT priorities by frontier uncertainty * L-hat per stratum.
algorithms.input: Time budget T, Tier 1 results, element count, parameter dimension, per-query costs

algorithms.name: Multi-Step Interaction Verification
algorithms.pseudocode_summary: For sequence (e_1,...,e_k), k<=3: sample IK solutions Q_i for each element e_i (100 samples each). Check feasibility of transitions via linear interpolation within J(theta). For certificate: use marginal stratification over Theta (d=5) only, not full trajectory space. Relaxed epsilon targets: 0.05 (k=1), 0.10 (k=2), 0.15 (k=3).
algorithms.input: Interaction sequence pi, body parameters theta, device d
algorithms.output: Accessibility verdict (sampling-based, may have false negatives)

implementation.risk: Medium: IK sampling coverage for k=3

algorithms.theorem_dependency: D4 (multi-step accessibility definition), C1 (extended to k-step)
algorithms.synthesis_modifications: Marginal stratification only for k>=2 (synthesis Critique 6); relaxed epsilon targets

algorithms.name: End-to-End Pipeline
algorithms.pseudocode_summary: Stage 1: Parse Unity scene -> extract interactables + dependency graph. Stage 2 (Tier 1): AffineFK over all elements -> GREEN/YELLOW/RED annotations. Stage 3 (Tier 2 only): Budget allocation -> adaptive sampling -> SMT targeting -> multi-step checks -> certificate assembly. Stage 4: Report generation with per-element verdicts, certificate, counterexamples.
algorithms.input: Unity scene path, target population, target devices, tier (1 or 2), time budget
algorithms.output: Accessibility report with certificate (Tier 2) or annotations (Tier 1)
algorithms.theorem_dependency: All theorems compose through the pipeline

hypotheses.statement: Coverage certificate epsilon improves over Clopper-Pearson by >=3x (hard pass) / >=5x (stretch)
hypotheses.test: Paired one-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank on epsilon ratios across N>=50 scenes
hypotheses.sample_size: 100 procedural scenes x 3 budgets = 300 paired observations
hypotheses.failure_action: Downscope to tool-only paper; certificates are supplementary result

hypotheses.statement: Affine-arithmetic wrapping factor <=5x on 4-joint +-30deg (D1a) and <=10x on 7-joint realistic ranges after subdivision (D1b)
hypotheses.test: One-sample one-sided t-test on log(w) across 1000 random chain geometries
hypotheses.failure_action: Taylor models fallback; if >10x with Taylor, ABANDON linter

hypotheses.statement: Tier 1 detection rate >95% and false positive rate <15%
hypotheses.failure_action: Revise subdivision strategy; if TPR <90%, Tier 1 is not viable

hypotheses.statement: Tier 2 detection rate >97% and false positive rate <5%
Formal verification detects >=10% of bugs missed by frontier-adaptive MC baseline
hypotheses.test: Binomial test on MC-missed bugs detected by formal verification
hypotheses.failure_action: Reframe: certification value (epsilon guarantee) rather than detection rate

hypotheses.statement: Multi-step (k<=3) certificates achieve epsilon <0.15 on 75% of benchmark scenes in 15 minutes
hypotheses.null_hypothesis: Fewer than 75% of scenes achieve epsilon <0.15
hypotheses.failure_action: Restrict to k<=2; defer k=3 to future work

hypotheses.statement: Certificate epsilon improves monotonically with compute budget (diminishing returns curve)
hypotheses.test: Spearman rank correlation across budgets T in {1, 5, 10, 30, 60} minutes

hypotheses.statement: Lipschitz violations occur in <20% of scenes and are detectable by the boundary heuristic
hypotheses.null_hypothesis: Violations in >=20% of scenes or detection rate <80%
hypotheses.failure_action: Downscope certificate applicability; report kappa honestly

benchmarks.procedural: 500+ scenes with controlled axes: object count (5,10,30,50,100) x spatial distribution (clustered, uniform, adversarial) x interaction depth (1-step, 2-step, 3-step) x anthropometric sensitivity (low/medium/high). Each scene generated deterministically from seed.
benchmarks.real_scenes: >=5 non-procedural: 3 from open-source (VR Interaction Framework samples, Meta Interaction SDK demos, A-Frame examples converted to Unity IR) + 2 hand-crafted (VR settings menu with nested panels; industrial control panel with multi-step procedures)
benchmarks.bug_injection: 8 categories (unreachable elements, occlusion deadlocks, pose-impossible gestures, cross-device failures, sequential traps, anthropometric exclusion, seated-mode failures, bimanual impossibilities) x 5 severity levels = 40 templates. 10% of procedural scenes have injected bugs.

baselines: Baseline 1: Stratified Monte Carlo (1M samples, stratified by ANSUR-II percentiles)
Baseline 2: Frontier-adaptive importance-sampled MC (strong baseline per synthesis Decision 9)
Baseline 3: Clopper-Pearson confidence intervals from MC samples
Baseline 4: Lookup table (20 discrete anthropometric percentiles, ~500 LoC)
Baseline 5: Geometric heuristic checker (bounding-box reachability, no state machines)
Baseline 6: Random walk exploration agent (simulated manual testing)
ablation_studies: A1: No SMT verification (sampling only) -> impact on epsilon
A2: No Tier 1 seeding -> impact on Tier 2 efficiency
A3: No Lipschitz detection -> impact on certificate validity
A4: No adaptive sampling (uniform only) -> impact on epsilon
A5: No subdivision in affine arithmetic -> impact on FPR
A6: No Tier 1 green regions in V (validates synthesis Decision 7)

kill_gates.criterion: D1a: wrapping <=5x on 4-joint +-30deg (95% of geometries). D1b: wrapping <=10x on 7-joint realistic ranges (+-60deg shoulder, +-70deg elbow, +-45deg wrist) after subdivision (90% of geometries).
kill_gates.fail_action: D1a fail: Taylor models fallback. D1b fail with subdivision: Taylor models. If Taylor >10x: ABANDON linter, lookup table only. No publication.

kill_gates.criterion: Certificate prototype demonstrates epsilon <0.10 on 10-object benchmark with 5-minute budget
kill_gates.fail_action: epsilon >0.10: ABANDON certificate framework. Publish Tier 1 linter as standalone UIST tool paper.

kill_gates.criterion: Certificate epsilon improves over Clopper-Pearson by >=3x (hard pass) on >=80% of scenes. Stretch: >=5x.
kill_gates.fail_action: <3x: Certificate adds marginal value. DOWNSCOPE to tool-only paper; certificate supplementary.

kill_gates.criterion: Lipschitz violation characterization: identify which scenes violate Lipschitz; measure frequency. Violations in <20% of scenes.
kill_gates.fail_action: >20% violations: DOWNSCOPE certificate applicability claim. Report as conditional result.

kill_gates.criterion: Publish Tier 1 linter as free Unity plugin. Survey >=20 XR developers. >=25% express concrete interest.
kill_gates.fail_action: <25%: Document as known limitation. Proceed with theory paper regardless.

kill_gates.criterion: Multi-step (k<=3) certificates achieve epsilon <0.15 on 75% of benchmark scenes within 15 minutes
kill_gates.fail_action: Restrict to k<=2 in paper. Defer k=3 to future work.

kill_gates.criterion: Formal verification detects >=10% of bugs missed by frontier-adaptive MC baseline
kill_gates.fail_action: 5-10%: Reframe as certification; Tier 3 only. <5%: DOWNSCOPE to theory paper + Tier 1 tool paper.

kill_gates.criterion: >=10 real XR scenes processed; >=5 complete Tier 2 analysis within budget
kill_gates.fail_action: <5 real scenes: DOWNSCOPE to procedural benchmarks only. Acknowledge limitation.

implementation_mapping.timeline: 9 months: Months 1-2 (Tier 1 linter + D1), Months 2-4 (sampling + SMT + D2/D3), Months 3-6 (certificates + D4/D5), Months 6-9 (evaluation + A3/A4 + papers)

critical_risks.risk: Certificate epsilon too loose (>0.05 on typical scenes)
critical_risks.impact: High: certificate framework provides marginal value over MC
critical_risks.mitigation: Tier 1 as verified volume (Decision 7); frontier-resolution; epsilon-vs-budget curve

critical_risks.risk: Wrapping factor exceeds 10x on realistic 7-joint chains
critical_risks.impact: High: Tier 1 linter false positive rate >50%
High: paper must reframe around structural advantages not epsilon tightness
critical_risks.mitigation: Relaxed to 3x hard pass; structural framing (spatial map, counterexamples)

critical_risks.risk: Zero developer demand for XR accessibility tools
critical_risks.impact: Medium: value proposition fails but theory paper survives
critical_risks.mitigation: D7 demand validation; theory paper independent of demand

risk_assessment.compound_risk: ~40-50% probability of at least one fatal flaw (reduced from 85% original estimate by abandoning SE(3) CAD approach)

escape_hatches.D1_fail: No publication for linter. Lookup table utility only.
escape_hatches.D2_fail: UIST tool paper on Tier 1 linter. Viable but thin.
escape_hatches.D3_fail: UIST tool paper (strong) + downscoped CAV paper (certificates supplementary).
escape_hatches.A3_A4_fail: CAV theory paper on coverage certificates (domain-independent) + UIST Tier 1 tool paper. Two modest papers.
escape_hatches.worst_case: Lookup table + theory publication on piecewise-Lipschitz certificate framework.

paper_1.title: Coverage Certificates for Parameter-Space Verification: Combining Statistical Sampling with Symbolic Proofs
paper_1.framing: General framework for verifying properties across parameterized families of systems. XR accessibility is motivating application; framework applies to robotics, medical devices, autonomous driving.
paper_1.acceptance_probability: 20-30% (conditional on epsilon being meaningfully tight at D3)
reviewer_anticipation: Lipschitz too strong -> piecewise-Lipschitz with kappa-completeness
How much better than sampling? -> D3 data, structural advantages
Linearization unsound at scale -> C2 bounds + empirical

paper_2.title: AccessLint-XR: Real-Time Spatial Accessibility Verification for Mixed Reality Development
paper_2.framing: Practical tool solving a real developer problem. Certificate is a CI/CD feature, not the intellectual centerpiece.

whats_new_vs_known.closest_prior: Statistical model checking (Younes & Simmons 2002; Legay et al. 2010)
whats_new_vs_known.whats_new: Deterministic parameter-space verification (not stochastic temporal properties); integration of interval-verified regions; piecewise-Lipschitz handling of joint-limit discontinuities; kappa-completeness metrics
Explicit constant C_FK = n/2 for revolute chains; coupling between body parameters and joint angles; application to accessibility SMT encoding

whats_new_vs_known.closest_prior: Interval analysis error bounds (Moore 1966); affine arithmetic (Stolfi & de Figueiredo 2003)
whats_new_vs_known.whats_new: Connection between wrapping factor and spatial accessibility detection margin; subdivision analysis

whats_new_vs_known.closest_prior: Affine arithmetic wrapping (Stolfi & de Figueiredo 2003); interval FK (Merlet 2004)
whats_new_vs_known.whats_new: Tight per-chain analysis with explicit multiplicative structure for revolute kinematic chains

whats_new_vs_known.closest_prior: Multi-fidelity optimization (Peherstorfer et al. 2018); adaptive reliability analysis (Au & Beck 2001)
whats_new_vs_known.whats_new: Application to certificate optimization; frontier-resolution model for SMT value

whats_new_vs_known.closest_prior: W3C XR Accessibility Requirements (2021); Reuleaux reachability maps (Zacharias et al. 2007)
whats_new_vs_known.whats_new: Formal scene model with dependency graph; parametric accessibility predicates; device-capability integration

synthesis_decisions: D1: Piecewise-Lipschitz with kappa-completeness (accepted, resolves Red-Team Attack 1.1)
D2: SMT frontier-resolution is conditional enhancement, not proven theorem (tested at D3)
D3: Tier 1 subdivision max 6 levels with mandatory joint-angle subdivision >45deg
D4: Multi-step limit k<=3 with marginal stratification and relaxed epsilon targets
D5: Budget 95%+ sampling, <=5% SMT default; adaptive alpha at runtime
D6: Certificate includes kappa-completeness with three tiers (Full/Partial/Weak)
D7: Tier 1 green regions count as verified volume — MOST IMPACTFUL cross-proposal insight
D8: Dual epsilon reporting: analytical (provably sound) + estimated (tight, cross-validated)
D9: Strong frontier-adaptive MC baseline required (replaces strawman stratified MC)
D10: D1 gate strengthened with D1b (7-joint realistic ranges)
